title: alnus incana f. acuminata
intro: Alnus incana f. acuminata, also known as the narrow-leaved alder, is a variant of the alder tree, specifically the green alder (Alnus incana) subspecies. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The narrow-leaved alder is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its bark and leaves can also be used in decoctions, infusions, and salves for topical application.
